questions.question: Explain the difference between Lambertian and specular reflection with examples of surfaces that exhibit each type.
expectedPoints: Lambertian reflection scatters light equally in all directions
Specular reflection reflects light in specific mirror-like directions
Matte surfaces (paper, wall paint) exhibit Lambertian reflection
Shiny surfaces (mirror, metal) exhibit specular reflection
Lambertian appearance is view-independent, specular is view-dependent

questions.question: Describe the complete process of photometric image formation from scene radiance to digital pixel values, including all major steps and transformations.
expectedPoints: Scene radiance determined by light sources and surface properties
Light passes through camera lens system with aperture control
Image irradiance equation relates scene radiance to sensor illumination
Spatial sampling converts continuous image to discrete grid
Quantization converts continuous intensity to discrete levels
Color filtering using Bayer pattern captures color information
Camera response function maps light to pixel values
Additional processing: demosaicing, white balance, gamma correction
